question: Davidson, Finnveden, and Hadshar describe AI-enabled coups (small groups seizing power within a country); Davidson separately argues a single country could outgrow the rest of the world via superexponential growth. How do these two power-concentration mechanisms interact? Could a successful coup in a leading AI country guarantee global outgrowth, or do the mechanisms operate at independent levels?

expected_citations.title: AI-Enabled Coups: How a Small Group Could Use AI to Seize Power
Could one country outgrow the rest of the world?

required_elements: AI-enabled coups describe intra-country power seizure by small groups using singularly-loyal AI
Outgrowth describes inter-country economic divergence under superexponential growth
The mechanisms are at different scales (intra-state political vs. inter-state economic)
Outgrowth requires sustained coordinated effort (e.g., chip export controls) - not automatic from coup success
Combined risk: a coup in a leading AI country could remove constraints on outgrowth-favouring policy
Synthesis: the two papers describe different but compatible pathways to extreme power concentration
